After the group departs, Oz is left alone. He drops the persona of the Great Wizard, admitting to himself that he is merely a humbug using sleight of hand. He expresses profound doubt about his ability to solve Dorothy's problem of returning home.
Grand, brightly lit throne room; Oz maintains formal posture.
Shadowy, intimate space; Oz is visibly anxious and doubting.
Oz sheds his public persona to admit his status as a humbug.
